title: Paper Crowns

inputs.title: Paper Crowns
inputs.theme: refusing the fake status games and crowning yourself on your own terms

brief: Make a hook first drill-influenced trap song titled "Paper Crowns". Center it on: refusing, fake, status, games, crowning, yourself, your, own. Keep it grounded and specific — not corny. Tempo 140-146 BPM. References (feel, never copied): none given.
conceptSummary: Drill-Influenced Trap record for the doubters: refusing the fake status games and crowning yourself on your own terms — delivered cold, confident, sharp, voiced as hard.

hookOptions.text: Still standing where the games used to be
Tell doubters I made it out the own
I been heavy but I never let it show
This one's for the games that raised me
For doubters, I climb through the own

production.bass: sub bass locked to the kick
arrangement: Intro: sparse, just the lead motif + vinyl
Hook: full drums in, 808 glide, double the vocal
Verse: pull the pad, let the knock breathe
Bridge: drop drums, swell, then re-enter on the one
Outro: filter the beat down, leave the hook ringing
production.genreBlend: drill-influenced trap core with melodic/emotional top line
production.mixVibe: wide, warm low end, vocal up front, slight tape saturation

vocals.delivery: cold, confident, sharp delivery, conversational on verses, lifted on the hook
vocals.doublesAndStacks: Double the hook, stack harmonies on the last line of each chorus.

visuals.albumCoverPrompt: Album cover, square 1:1. Cinematic, cold, confident, sharp mood. Subject reflects: Drill-Influenced Trap record for the doubters: refusing the fake status games and crowning yourself on your own terms — delivered cold, confident, sharp, voiced as hard.. Visual motifs: refusing, fake, status, games. Color: deep shadows, one warm accent light. Film grain, 35mm. No text, no logos, no real-artist likeness. Original styling.
visuals.musicVideoPrompt: Cinematic 16:9 music video. Concept: Drill-Influenced Trap record for the doubters: refusing the fake status games and crowning yourself on your own terms — delivered cold, confident, sharp, voiced as hard.. cold, confident, sharp tone, drill-influenced trap energy. Grounded, not corny; real locations, natural light with one stylized accent. Original — no copyrighted footage or artist mimicry.
sceneIdeas: Opening: slow 16:9 push-in on the subject, cold, confident, sharp light, city at dawn.
Hook: handheld, performance energy, golden hour.
Bridge: single locked-off wide, subject still while the world moves.
Outro: pull back to reveal the place it all started.

Single-take walk synced to the bridge

data.brief: Make a hook first drill-influenced trap song titled "Paper Crowns". Center it on: refusing, fake, status, games, crowning, yourself, your, own. Keep it grounded and specific — not corny. Tempo 140-146 BPM. References (feel, never copied): none given.
data.concept: Drill-Influenced Trap record for the doubters: refusing the fake status games and crowning yourself on your own terms — delivered cold, confident, sharp, voiced as hard.

angles.angle: Tell "refusing" through a letter you'll never send
angles.why: An oblique frame forces fresh, specific images instead of the obvious ones.

angles.angle: Anchor the whole song to one image: "road"
angles.why: A single concrete anchor from an unrelated domain (street) gives the song a spine and a surprise.

angles.angle: Flip the perspective — write it as a conversation that never happened
angles.why: Re-narrating from an unexpected vantage is the DMN move that lifts originality.
